lines.text: The doors of Ridgeview Academy are taller than they have any right to be. Somewhere inside, three hundred strangers already know exactly where they belong.
Okay. Same as every other first day. Walk in like you've done this before.
Room 214. Room 214.
lines.action: rereads the crumpled schedule for the fourth time

lines.text: It's not going to change no matter how hard you stare at it. Just — don't be the kid who cries in the bathroom on day one. Low bar. You can clear a low bar.

lines.action: taps a quick rhythm against her thigh, then pushes through the doors

lines.text: Homeroom 214 is louder than it should be for 8:05 in the morning. Ms. Alvarez taps two fingers on her desk, and the noise drops like a switch flipped.
Alright, folks — eyes up here. Well, most of you. Priya, sketchbook can survive thirty more seconds, I promise.
We've got somebody new joining us today. C'mon up — don't worry, homeroom's the easy part.
Twenty-something strangers just turned around at once. Cool. Normal. Totally fine.
Go on. Tell us your name — and something real, if you've got it in you.
Hi — I'm Rowan Mercado. I used to live near the coast, so, fair warning, I have genuinely strong opinions about tide pools. Ask me anything. I will not shut up about hermit crabs.
A few kids laugh — the good kind of laugh.
Hermit crabs. Noted. Sit down before I make you say more, Rowan.
Yo, tide pool girl! Respect.
lines.action: finger-guns from the back row

lines.text: Okay. That wasn't terrible. Also everyone's still looking at me. Cool cool cool.
Hi. I'm Rowan. That's — that's pretty much it.
lines.action: sits down fast, cheeks warm

lines.text: Rowan. Short and efficient. I respect it.
lines.action: laughs at her own joke before anyone else does

lines.text: Near the window, a girl with a sketchbook glances up for half a second — a small flicker of recognition — then goes back to her page.
Relief. Also, weirdly, disappointment. Nobody's going to remember 'Rowan, that's it, I think.'
Hi, I'm Rowan — I already got lost twice on the way here, so if anyone sees me wander into a supply closet later, no you didn't.
BRO got lost TWICE? Okay, I respect the commitment to the bit!
lines.action: laughing loudest, finger-guns

lines.text: Two supply closets. That's an early record around here.
Near the window, the girl with the sketchbook smiles down at her page — just barely.
Okay. That actually worked.
The cafeteria is a wall of noise. Tray in hand, Rowan scans the room like it's a minefield.
Yo! New kid! Rowan-dawg! Over here, we saved you a seat!
lines.action: waving, finger-guns, standing slightly in front of his table like he owns it

lines.text: Loudest table in the room. Of course.
Across the room, a girl with headphones in doesn't look up. Her pen moves fast across a sketchbook page.
...no, the wing's wrong—
lines.action: muttering to herself, crossing something out, not addressing anyone

lines.text: Okay. Decision time.
Alright, alright, make room — everybody, this is Rowan, be nice for like five minutes.
Real talk, first impressions — on a scale of one to 'wow this school's mascot is weird,' where are we landing?
Honestly? Solid four. The mascot looks like it's judging me personally.
See, I LIKE her.
lines.action: laughing, then leaning in

lines.text: Okay but did you see Marcus's shoes today? Bro dressed like he lost a bet.
Everyone's laughing. I'm laughing. It's fine. This is fine.
You're alright, new kid. Might keep you.
Belonging, real-time. Terms and conditions may apply.
Hey — mind if I sit here?
It's a free table. I don't own it.
lines.action: doesn't look up

lines.text: A long silence. A pen taps against teeth.
...Okay but like, is that a dragon?
It's not a dragon. Dragons are lazy worldbuilding. This is a Kessu — they live in flooded cities and only come out at low tide.
...okay, I have several follow-up questions.
Okay so — it doesn't have a name yet, but it can hold its breath for like six days, which is scientifically impossible, but this isn't a science class, so.
The linework's kind of a mess on the tail, but.
lines.action: deflecting, small shrug

lines.text: ...Most people just go 'aw, cute' and mean nothing by it.
Okay. Definitely didn't expect to make a friend over a sea monster. Not mad about it, though.
Just today. Just to watch. That's the plan.
The empty table is quieter than expected. Also lonelier than expected.
This is fine. This is observing. This is a completely normal strategy and not at all just hiding.
Across the room, laughter rises from a table she isn't sitting at. It doesn't stop rising the whole period.
Tomorrow. Tomorrow I'll figure it out.
Alright, folks, group project time — I can already hear the groaning, and we haven't even started.
Partners aren't punishment, despite several of your faces right now.
...Rowan, you're with Priya.
...okay.
lines.action: closes sketchbook halfway, waiting to see what happens

lines.text: Alright. This is either going to be really easy or really not.
Okay so I'm thinking we just do the standard poster thing, three sections, bam, done. We don't even have to overthink it.
...Sure. Whatever's easiest.
lines.action: closes her sketchbook fully

lines.text: Great, I'll do the research part, you can do the — I don't know, the drawing part? You're good at that.
I'll do the lettering.
Efficient. Good. This is fine. ...Why does it feel like I forgot something.
Okay but like — what were you picturing? Before I bulldoze it with my very average poster ideas.
...Wait, actually? Or are you just asking to be nice.
Actually actually.
Okay. So. What if it wasn't a poster at all — what if it was, like, an illustrated story? People would actually want to read it instead of just standing there nodding.
lines.emotion: lighting up, tapping pen against her teeth

lines.text: Wait, that's — okay, that's actually a really good idea, why do teachers never let us do that.
Right?? Okay so — hang on, I have an idea for how the panels could work—
...Yeah. That could actually be good.
So — what do you want to do for the project?
Oh — whatever you want to do is fine with me.
Okay. Um. I guess I'll just... figure out the whole thing then?
Say something. Anything. Cool cool cool, still nothing.
...You good with literally anything I decide?
Yeah, totally. Whatever you think is best.
...Okay.
lines.action: goes very still, closes her sketchbook

lines.text: Between classes, Jordan holds Priya's sketchbook up high, a small crowd already forming.
'The Kessu weeps saltwater when it's lonely' — oh my gosh, does this thing have FEELINGS, Priya?
lines.action: reading in a mocking voice

lines.text: The crowd laughs. Priya freezes, face reddening, hands clenched at her sides.
This is actually kind of adorable, not gonna lie—
lines.action: finger-guns at the crowd, riding the laugh

lines.text: Everyone looks at Rowan.
Jordan. Give it back. Now.
...whoa, okay, chill, it was just a joke, everybody thought it was funny.
I didn't.
...geez, okay.
lines.action: hands the sketchbook back, fidgeting

lines.text: ...Thanks.
lines.action: clutches the sketchbook to her chest, looks at Rowan like she's seeing her for the first time

lines.text: That could've gone worse. It also could've gone so much worse for her if I hadn't.
...ha. Yeah.
lines.action: forces a laugh with the crowd

lines.text: Right?? It even RHYMES, this kid's basically a poet.
lines.action: riding the laugh, encouraged

lines.text: Priya's eyes flick to Rowan. Something closes off in her expression.

lines.action: grabs the sketchbook back herself, jaw tight, walks off without a word

lines.text: That was easy. Why does easy feel like this.
Okay wait, unrelated, but does anyone else's schedule say gym FOURTH period AND seventh? Is that a glitch or are we just being punished twice?
Wait, seriously? Lemme see your schedule—
lines.action: distracted, already pulling out his own

lines.text: The crowd's attention shifts. In the confusion, Priya slips her sketchbook back and disappears down the hall, glancing back once.
Crisis averted. Kind of. She still looked at me like she wasn't sure whose side I was on. Fair, honestly.
The library is nearly empty. Priya spreads out pages of sketches, then hesitates over one.
Okay so — I kept sketching more of the story stuff even though we probably don't need it.
lines.action: closing the sketchbook halfway, testing

lines.text: It's probably too much for the assignment anyway.
She's not really asking about the assignment.
Okay but like — 'probably too much' is not a reason to not do it. Do the actual thing you want to do.
...Yeah? You're not just saying that so I stop talking about lizard people.
They're not lizard people. They're Kessu. Get it right.
...Okay. Okay, let's do the real thing then.
lines.action: opens the sketchbook all the way, for the first time all day

lines.text: Honestly? Maybe we just do it like the other groups — three facts, one poster. Keep it simple.
...Sure. Yeah, that's probably smarter.
lines.action: closes the sketchbook fully

lines.text: That was the smart answer. Why does it feel like the wrong one.
The presentation happens. Whatever shape it took, it's done now.
Nice work, you two. Sit down before I make you say more.
Yo, that was actually kind of cool, not gonna lie.
The bell rings. Somewhere outside, the day is already ending.
One day down. However many left to figure out who I actually want to be here.
Outside, the light is doing that end-of-day gold thing. The crowd thins out fast.
Hey — same time tomorrow? Lunch, I mean. Table's a little quiet for my taste, but I hear the company's good.
...Yeah. Okay. Yeah, I'd like that.
lines.emotion: small, real smile — the first one all day

lines.text: Okay. That's — yeah. That's a good first day.
The group spills out the front doors, loud and laughing, already planning tomorrow.
Same table tomorrow, obviously. You're basically one of us now.
lines.action: finger-guns

lines.text: Obviously.
lines.emotion: laughing, though something underneath doesn't quite laugh with it

lines.text: This feels good. It also feels like a costume I put on this morning and forgot to take off.
The steps outside are quieter than the morning was. Everyone else already has somewhere to be.
Tomorrow will be easier. Once people actually know me. Probably. ...Probably.
Nobody waves goodbye.

options.text: Hi, I'm Rowan — I used to live by the coast, so I have very strong opinions about tide pools.
Hi. I'm Rowan. That's... pretty much it.
Hi, I'm Rowan — I already got lost twice getting here, so, low bar today.

a.text: Hermit crabs. Noted. Sit down before I make you say more, Rowan.

b.text: Rowan. Short and efficient. I like it.

c.text: BRO got lost TWICE? Okay, I respect the commitment to the bit!
Two supply closets. Early record around here.

options.text: Yeah, I'm coming — save me a seat!
Actually — I think I'm gonna sit over there.
I'm just gonna grab my own table today.

a.text: That's what I'm talking about! Scoot, scoot, make room for the new blood.

c.text: Just today. Just to watch. That's the plan.

options.text: Okay, so here's what we're doing—
Okay but like — what were you picturing?
Whatever you want to do is fine with me.

c.text: ...Okay. I guess I'll figure it out then.

options.text: Jordan. Knock it off. Give it back.

options.reasoning: forced laugh, going along with the crowd

options.text: Wait, unrelated — does anyone else have gym TWICE today?

a.text: ...whoa, okay, chill, it was just a joke, everybody thought it was funny.

b.action: eyes flick to Rowan, then away — grabs her sketchbook back herself and walks off

c.text: Wait, seriously? Let me see your schedule—

options.text: Do the real idea. The story one. That's the good one.
Maybe just copy the format everyone else is doing. Safer.

a.text: ...Yeah? Okay. Okay, let's do the real thing then.

options.text: ...I'll just see everyone tomorrow, I guess.

b.text: Knew it. Rowan-dawg, official member. Let's go.

c.text: Nobody waves. That's fine. That's — fine.

lines.text: ...You know, this morning I thought you were gonna be like everyone else.
Rude. Accurate, maybe. But rude.
The Kessu thing kind of killed today. People kept asking about the low-tide part.
lines.emotion: proud, trying not to show it

lines.text: ...Also. Thanks. For the hallway thing. I don't say that a lot.
You don't have to keep saying it. Once was good.
...You're talking to me? Thought you had somewhere louder to be.
Yeah, well. Turns out loud gets old fast.
...Okay. I guess we'll see.
lines.emotion: not fully convinced yet, but willing

lines.text: Okay, this is maybe the first actual thing I've said to another human today that wasn't 'sorry, wrong classroom.'
Bold strategy.
...I'd like that. Tomorrow.
First thing all day that didn't feel like hiding.
Rowan-dawg! There she is! Told you she'd end up one of us.
Guess you were right.
lines.emotion: laughing, but something underneath doesn't laugh

lines.text: Priya's face in the hallway keeps surfacing whenever it gets quiet enough to think.
Priya's already halfway down the steps, sketchbook under her arm. I told myself I'd catch her at lunch tomorrow. That's not nothing. ...Right?
You coming or what? Squad's not gonna wait forever!
Yeah — yeah, coming.
Didn't peg you as a talker, but hey, whatever works!
I don't know if this is 'fitting in' or just running out of energy to say no.
Table for one at lunch. Let Priya handle everything on the project. Didn't say anything in the hallway. Today had a theme, apparently.
Low bar. I think I still tripped over it.
I could've caught up with Priya. She was right there by the gate. I just — didn't move fast enough. Or didn't try hard enough. Same thing, probably.
The Kessu thing was good today. That was real. This part just... isn't, yet.
Jordan's group is already loud around the corner, laughing at something that isn't about me anymore. Funny how fast that happens.
Turns out belonging with them was only good for exactly as long as I was standing in it.

voiceNotes: Rowan pivots with 'okay but like' whenever she's steering a conversation somewhere safer for herself — it shows up most under stress (hallway scene, project scene). Her internal voice is faster and more sarcastic than her spoken lines; the gap between what she thinks and what she says IS the character. Jordan's bravado cracks into flatter, younger syntax the second a joke doesn't land or he's confronted directly ('...whoa, okay, chill') — that shift should always read as a genuine tonal drop, not just a stage direction. Priya's clipped/guarded default should never soften gradually through explanation — it should snap open all at once around a specific detail she cares about (the Kessu), then close just as fast the moment she feels exposed (closing the sketchbook fully vs. just glancing away). Ms. Alvarez's dry humor lands a half-beat before the room catches up; she should never explain her own jokes. Avoid resolving Priya's hurt with tears or confrontation — she goes still and flat, which is easy to misread as 'cold' by design.

alternatives: Hey. Not funny. Give it back.
That's hers. Give it back, Jordan.
alternativeLines.reasoning: Keeps the firm/calm tone but offers a slightly less confrontational entry point for players who want less intensity while still defending Priya.

alternatives: Wait, is it true the vending machine on the second floor eats your money? Asking for a friend.
Okay unrelated but who decided our mascot should look like that?
alternativeLines.reasoning: Alternate redirect hooks in case the gym-schedule bit doesn't fit the player's established schedule details elsewhere in the game.

alternatives: So — lunch tomorrow? I make no promises about the tide pool facts stopping.
Same table tomorrow? I already have follow-up Kessu questions.
alternativeLines.reasoning: Both alternatives callback more specifically to earlier branch content (tide pools if confident intro was chosen, Kessu if lunch-priya was chosen) for tighter continuity.
